snippet: Schools must have processes in place to ensure the security of all school-managed ICT assets throughout their lifecycle. This must include: Schools must properly dispose of all computer equipment to protect school and personal information from unauthorised access, such as exposure to the public. Computer equipment includes any computerised equipment that stores school or personal information. Devices defined as computer equipment include laptops, desktop computers, media storage devices (for example, hard drives and USB drives), printers, faxes, security systems and network devices. The equipment may be owned or leased by the school or may be staff-owned devices used for school purposes. As part of the disposal process: It is also essential that the Records Management and Software and Administration Systems policies are followed as part of the disposal process.
